chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to create log files using the Rheonics Control Panel software, detailing the steps and illustrating the process with a screenshot of the software interface.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
How to create log files?
Modified on: Thu, 28 Apr, 2022 at 4:54 PM

1. How to create log files?
Logging data points is an useful tool in the process management, since this measure data can be used for further analysis and review.
1.1. Go to the “Measurement” tab and to the “Log Measurements” box.

Rheonics Control Panel S.3.1.6
Measurement | Events | Settings | Service | Diagnostics | Communication | Scaling | Calculation | Logger | Help

Measured Values
Viscosity: 0.050 mPa.s
Density: 1.00 g/cc
Temperature: 23.68 °C
Pressure: 1.00 bar

Status
Running ... E00
Logging: Stopped
Pressure: Manual
Manual entry: 1 bar

Log Measurements
Sample Name:
Start Logging
Add Event
...

Sensor Information
Serial Number: BJBW-B00-0000
Fres: 7419.73 Hz
df: 1.49 Hz

Figure 1. Log Measurement Box, in this place is created the log file
key_facts: Logging data points is an useful tool in process management for further analysis and review.
To create log files, users should navigate to the 'Measurement' tab and then the 'Log Measurements' box in the Rheonics Control Panel software.
The document was last modified on Thursday, April 28, 2022, at 4:54 PM.
The software version depicted is Rheonics Control Panel S.3.1.6.

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software (version 5.3.1.6) is used to control and log data from sensors.
Users must enter a sample name into the 'Sample Name' box before initiating data logging.
Clicking the 'Start Logging' button will prompt the user to specify a file path for saving the log data.
The software displays real-time measurements for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure.
Sensor information, including serial number (BJBw-B00-0000), resonant frequency (Fres), and frequency delta (df), is displayed.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to save measurement data logs using the Rheonics Control Panel software, including details on the default file naming convention and a visual representation of the software interface during logging.
chunks.extracted_text:
1.4. The default name is YYYYMMDDHHMMSS where the first 8 characters are a date code, and the next 6 are a time code. This can be changed to a user provided name. Click “ok” and a data log file will be created Software will start recording the measurement data in that file.

Figure 4. Selecting file path to save data log.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.3.1.6

key_facts: The default data log file name uses the YYYYMMDDHHMMSS format, where the first 8 characters represent a date code and the next 6 represent a time code.
Users can change the default data log file name to a custom name.
Clicking 'OK' in the save dialog creates a data log file and initiates the software to record measurement data into it.
The Rheonics Control Panel software displays measured values for Viscosity, Density, Temperature, and Pressure.
The software's status panel indicates connection errors (e.g., E99) and whether logging is running.
Pressure can be set to 'Manual' with a manual entry value.
The software shows the path and name of the log file being created, for example, C:\Users\...net\008230622_test1_log.txt.
Sensor information includes fields for Serial Number (sn) and Fres (df).

chunks.page_summary: This page details how to create an event and snapshot within the Rheonics Control Panel software, illustrating the process with a screenshot of the user interface and accompanying instructions.
chunks.extracted_text:
2. Creating an Event and Snapshot
2.1. At any time if need to be, events can be added to a log file - for example a change in the measurement condition or modifying the fluid under test. To create an event, click the button “Add Event”

Rheonics Control Panel 5.3.1.6

Menu Tabs:
Measurement
Events
Settings
Service
Diagnostics
Communication
Scaling
Calculation
Logger
Help

Measured Values:
Viscosity: 0.051 mPa.s
Density: 1.00 g/cc
Temperature: 23.68 °C
Pressure: 1.00 bar

Status:
Running ... E00
Logging: Running
Pressure: Manual
Manual entry: 1 bar

Log Measurements:
Sample Name: test1
Stop Logging (button)
Add Event (button)
... (text field)

Sensor Information:
Serial Number: BJBW-B00-0000
Fres: 7419.75 Hz
df: 1.48 Hz

Figure 6. Click the “Add Event” to create an event in the log file.
key_facts: Events can be added to a log file to mark changes in measurement conditions or fluid modifications.
To create an event, the user must click the “Add Event” button.
The software interface shows real-time measurements for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure.
The system is currently running and logging data.

2.3.Check the event has been registered in the “Events” tab.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.3.1.6
Measurement Events Settings Service Diagnostics Communication Scaling Calculation Logger Help

Events (tab content)
Date Time Tag Description
10/8/2021 23:06:38 Event_1 This is a test

Last Received String From SME
BJBW-B00-0000 03.20/4-g0bc7d04 19101698 T 23.676001 f 7419.766113 df 1.511719 Fv 541 ph 22 V 0.047928 D 1.000000 P 1.000000 fc 7419.766113 I- 1 I+ 1 Q_0.981189 fr 7419.750977 df- 7419.012695 df+ 7420.503906 Tc nan Tp 23.674999 Te 63.202148 E 00 A 3
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel version 5.3.1.6 is used for monitoring and logging sensor data.
The system displays real-time measurements for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure.

Users can add custom event tags with descriptions, timestamps, and dates (e.g., 10/8/2021 at 23:06:38 with tag 'Event_1' and description 'This is a test') to mark specific process states or observations.
Logged events are recorded and viewable in the 'Events' tab of the control panel, showing date, time, tag, and description.
The system provides sensor information including serial number, resonant frequency (Fres), and frequency differential (df).
Logging status indicates the system is currently 'Running', and pressure control is set to 'Manual'.

A detailed 'Last Received String From SME' provides raw sensor data including values for temperature (T), frequency (f), delta frequency (df), fluid viscosity (Fv), phase (ph), voltage (V), density (D), pressure (P), corrected frequency (fc), and other parameters.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to create a snapshot of logged measurement data and how to stop the logging process within a software interface, accompanied by screenshots.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 8. Event tag added to “Events” tab.

2.4. Sometimes in long logging runs, it is desirable to see a “snapshot” of the data collected up to a given time without interrupting the logging process. This can be done by clicking on the button to the right of the log file path box.

Log Measurements
Sample Name: test1
Stop Logging
Add Event
-211014122100_test1_log.txt

Figure 9. Snapshot button

2.5. Once the “Snapshot” button is click, the “Measurement log file copy” menu will open showing the buttons in figure 10.

Measurement log file copy
Created the following copy of the measurement log file:
C:\Users\...ntrolPanel\10142021_1421_ - no sn - _211014122100_test1_log.txt
Open File
Open Folder
Close

Figure 10. Log file copy after taking snapshot

3. Stop Logging

3.1. Clicking the “Stop Logging” button will stop the logging.
key_facts: A snapshot of collected data can be obtained during long logging runs without interrupting the logging process by clicking a button next to the log file path box.
Clicking the snapshot button opens a 'Measurement log file copy' menu.
The 'Measurement log file copy' menu displays the path to the copied log file and offers 'Open File', 'Open Folder', and 'Close' options.
Clicking the 'Stop Logging' button will terminate the logging process.
diagram_labels: Created the following copy of the measurement log file:

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software version is 5.3.1.6.
The software displays real-time measured values for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure.
Data logging is active and running, with a sample named 'test1' in progress.
The system is connected to a sensor with serial number BJBw-B00-0000.
Manual pressure entry is set to 1 bar.
The software creates three types of log files: debug, events, and a main log file, all timestamped and including the sensor's serial number and sample name.
The provided timestamps indicate data logging occurred around 22:54:56 to 23:28:15, and log files were modified at 10/8/2021 11:06 PM.
Graphs visualize the trends of viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure over time.

3.5. The debug file, shows the string that is sent from the serial port or ethernet communication:
key_facts: Log files record system configuration details such as start log time, sensor type, serial numbers, communication mode, operator information, and software version.
The log file contains time-series data points including relative timestamp, viscosity measurements, and density readings.
The event file lists events, which can be user-created or alarms.
The debug file captures raw communication strings sent via serial port or ethernet.
The Rheonics Control Panel software version used in this log is 5.3.1.6.
The sensor's communication mode is USB (COM18).
The sensor's type is SRV with serial number BJB-B00-0000 and SME serial number 19101698.
The log data shows a consistent viscosity of 23.68 mPa.s and an undefined density (NaN).
diagram_labels: Figure 13. Data points in Log file
